meta_title: For Freelancers: client workflow to Solve can't finish projects (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to client workflow and overcome can't finish projects. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Client Workflow to Solve Project Stagnation
content.seo_description: Stop leaving projects unfinished. Master this high-efficiency client workflow using Antigravity and Claude Code to ship faster and impress your clients.
content.answer_block: To stop leaving projects unfinished, you must shift from 'coding' to 'engineering systems.' By implementing a standardized client workflow, you constrain scope, automate repetitive boilerplate, and utilize AI-driven CLI tools like Claude Code to maintain velocity. This approach turns project completion into a predictable, repeatable, and high-margin technical output.
content.intro:
You know the feeling: the initial spark of a new project is electric, but as the deadline looms, the codebase becomes a graveyard of half-baked features and mounting technical debt. You aren't failing because you lack skill; you are failing because your workflow lacks a structural backbone. 

Professional shipping is not about raw effort—it is about rigorous systems. By integrating Antigravity for architectural scaffolding and Claude Code for rapid execution, you can transform your freelance practice from a chaotic struggle into a high-velocity production engine. It’s time to stop chasing the 'perfect' build and start delivering the 'finished' product.

steps.title: Define Strict Scoping Constraints
steps.description: Before writing code, use Antigravity to generate a feature-locked architecture that prevents scope creep and ensures you only build what is on the contract.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a lean technical specification document for a SaaS MVP. Focus on core user flow, data schema, and API endpoints. Exclude all 'nice-to-have' features.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate project_spec.md based on user_requirements.txt'

steps.title: Automate Boilerplate Scaffolding
steps.description: Stop setting up folders and auth manually. Use AI to generate the entire project skeleton so you can focus on the business logic immediately.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a full-stack Next.js 14 project skeleton with TypeScript, Tailwind, and Supabase integration. Include a directory structure for clean architecture.

steps.title: Build by Feature Modules
steps.description: Break the project into atomic, deployable modules. This keeps you focused on small wins rather than the overwhelming 'whole' project.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Decompose the user authentication module into 3 sub-tasks: UI, database schema, and validation logic. Provide the code for the database schema.

steps.title: Continuous Code Review
steps.description: Use Claude Code to audit your work as you write it, catching bugs before they become blockers that kill your momentum.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current codebase for potential security vulnerabilities and performance bottlenecks. Suggest 3 refactors to improve readability.

steps.title: Automate the Handover
steps.description: Finalize your project with an automated documentation generation step that makes your work look premium and professional to the client.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a professional README.md, API documentation, and a client deployment guide based on the implemented features.

pro_tips: Always commit your work every 30 minutes; momentum is lost when you lose progress.
Use Claude Code's 'chat' mode to explain complex logic to your client, turning your code into a communication tool.
Never start a new feature until the previous one is fully covered by a unit test.
Maintain a 'kill list' of features that provide low ROI to the client; present it to them to save time.
Standardize your environment variables across all projects to reduce 'it works on my machine' friction.
content.next_steps: Select one active project currently stalling in your dashboard, apply the scoping constraint prompt from Step 1, and commit to shipping one module by the end of the day.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting half-finished code sit on your hard drive. Master the workflow that separates hobbyists from high-earning professional AI builders. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
